meta.title: MCP | kalandra.tech — Talk to This Site from Your AI Assistant
meta.description: Connect Claude or any MCP-compatible AI assistant to kalandra.tech: browse the blog without an account, and with sign-in submit job offers and write comments — over the Model Context Protocol.

hero.description: This site runs its own Model Context Protocol server. Connect it to Claude or any MCP-compatible assistant and it can browse the blog and read the comments right away — no account needed. Sign in, and it can also act here on your behalf: submit a job offer, follow the conversation about it, and join the comments. Every action goes through the same public API the website itself uses.

connect.title: Connect
connect.transportNote: Streamable HTTP, stateless. Sign-in is OAuth — there's no token to copy, and none to refresh when it expires.
connect.authDescription: Connecting needs no account — the blog tools work right away. The account tools appear once you sign the server in from your assistant's connector settings (in Claude Code: /mcp → kalandra → Authenticate). The sign-in is OAuth: your browser opens kalandra.tech, you approve once what the assistant may do, and it stays connected as your account — nothing to paste, and it never sees your password.

tools.name: submit_job_offer
tools.description: Send me a job offer — the same flow as the Hire Me form.

tools.name: list_my_job_offers
tools.description: Your submitted offers with their current status.

tools.name: get_job_offer_comments
tools.description: The conversation thread on one of your offers.

tools.name: add_job_offer_comment
tools.description: Reply in that thread — I get notified by email.

tools.name: list_blog_posts
tools.description: Published posts with summaries, tags, links to the full text, and each post's view, reaction, and comment counts. Signed in, each post also shows whether you've already read it.

tools.name: get_blog_post_comments
tools.description: The public comment thread of any post.

tools.name: post_blog_comment
tools.description: Comment on a post, or reply to someone, as you.

tools.name: get_my_comments
tools.description: Everything you've said here — blog and job-offer comments — with the replies they received.

examples.title: Try asking
prompts: Submit a job offer to Pavel: senior .NET engineer at Acme, remote, salary 120–150k.
What has Pavel written on his blog lately?
Did anyone reply to my comments on kalandra.tech?

how.title: How it works
items: The tools are a second front door onto the same domain: each call runs the same code the website's own requests do — the public ones anonymously, the account ones as you — with the same validation, rate limits, and email notifications.
Blog posts are read from the site's RSS feed, so the assistant sees exactly what is published.
Built in .NET with the official MCP C# SDK, running as its own host — separate from the REST API because it authenticates you with OAuth rather than a token the site hands out.
